acceptance.proof: Runtime activation, current fixtures, workspace registry, and deterministic accepted revision-14 lock all bind the same exact fingerprint while the prior fingerprint rejects.
Default inertness, module selection, dependency closure, current admission, and stale fingerprint rejection remain closed and unchanged.

commit_policy: Commit lifecycle separately; keep the semantic implementation delta to one fingerprint constant and refresh only current evidence/tests/routes; never rewrite historical receipts.

non_scope: Changing feature.lock.json, project.spec.json, selected modules, revision, default activation, activation rule, dependency closure, API shape, runtime behavior beyond exact accepted-lock admission, historical receipts, LSLC-004H evidence, devices, networking, unsafe/FFI, dependencies, commands, routing, admission, or Manifold authority.

outputs: One exact constant rebinding to the accepted deterministic revision-14 fingerprint.
